stoic.author: Marcus Aurelius
stoic.text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

stoic.author: Marcus Aurelius
stoic.text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.

stoic.author: Marcus Aurelius
stoic.text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.

stoic.author: Marcus Aurelius
stoic.text: When you arise in the morning, think of what a precious privilege it is to be alive — to breathe, to think, to enjoy, to love.

stoic.author: Marcus Aurelius
stoic.text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.

stoic.author: Marcus Aurelius
stoic.text: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, and do so with all your heart.

stoic.author: Epictetus
stoic.text: It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.

stoic.author: Epictetus
stoic.text: First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do.

stoic.author: Epictetus
stoic.text: No man is free who is not master of himself.

stoic.author: Epictetus
stoic.text: Make the best use of what is in your power, and take the rest as it happens.

stoic.author: Epictetus
stoic.text: He who laughs at himself never runs out of things to laugh at.

stoic.author: Seneca
stoic.text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.

stoic.author: Seneca
stoic.text: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.

stoic.author: Seneca
stoic.text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a great deal of it.

stoic.author: Seneca
stoic.text: Hang on to your youthful enthusiasms — you'll be able to use them better when you're older.

stoic.author: Seneca
stoic.text: Sometimes even to live is an act of courage.

stoic.author: Seneca
stoic.text: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.

heroes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
heroes.text: All that is gold does not glitter, not all those who wander are lost.

heroes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
heroes.text: I will not say: do not weep; for not all tears are an evil.

heroes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
heroes.text: There is some good in this world, Mr. Frodo, and it's worth fighting for.

heroes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
heroes.text: It is not despair, for despair is only for those who see the end beyond all doubt. We do not.

heroes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
heroes.text: Faithless is he that says farewell when the road darkens.

heroes.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
heroes.text: The world is indeed full of peril, and in it there are many dark places; but still there is much that is fair.

heroes.author: G.K. Chesterton
heroes.text: The world will never starve for want of wonders; but only for want of wonder.

heroes.author: G.K. Chesterton
heroes.text: Fairy tales do not tell children the dragons exist. Children already know that dragons exist. Fairy tales tell children the dragons can be killed.

heroes.author: G.K. Chesterton
heroes.text: The true soldier fights not because he hates what is in front of him, but because he loves what is behind him.

heroes.author: G.K. Chesterton
heroes.text: Hope is the power of being cheerful in circumstances which we know to be desperate.

heroes.author: G.K. Chesterton
heroes.text: When it comes to life, the critical thing is whether you take things for granted or take them with gratitude.

heroes.author: C.S. Lewis
heroes.text: You can't go back and change the beginning, but you can start where you are and change the ending.

heroes.author: C.S. Lewis
heroes.text: Hardships often prepare ordinary people for an extraordinary destiny.

heroes.author: C.S. Lewis
heroes.text: There are far, far better things ahead than any we leave behind.

heroes.author: C.S. Lewis
heroes.text: Getting over a painful experience is much like crossing monkey bars. You have to let go at some point in order to move forward.

heroes.author: C.S. Lewis
heroes.text: Integrity is doing the right thing, even when no one is watching.

heroes.author: C.S. Lewis
heroes.text: We are not necessarily doubting that God will do the best for us; we are wondering how painful the best will turn out to be.

recovery.author: Bill W.
recovery.text: We are going to know a new freedom and a new happiness.

recovery.author: AA Saying
recovery.text: The only requirement for membership is a desire to stop.

recovery.author: Reinhold Niebuhr
recovery.text: God, grant me the serenity to accept the things I cannot change, courage to change the things I can, and wisdom to know the difference.
